untranslatable_reason: This signal requires real-time monitoring of macroeconomic variables (inflation rate, interest rate changes, economic growth data) and qualitative trader judgment about causal chains. These are not price/technical indicators available in the backtester. The rule explicitly rejects mechanical formulas and pattern recognition in favor of fundamental thesis-based discretionary trading. Cannot be mechanically encoded with price data alone.
translation_notes: SCHWAGER_DRY_0_2 is a philosophical stance against mechanical trading systems rather than an actionable technical rule. Core requirements: (1) Observable economic signals (inflation spike >5%, rate hike >25bps) — not available as price indicators; (2) Trader articulation of causal mechanism — subjective judgment; (3) Conviction-based entry/exit tied to thesis invalidation — requires discretion. The 15% drawdown stop_loss is the only quantifiable parameter that could be mechanically applied, but without the fundamental trigger conditions, this becomes a generic risk rule. Historical backtesting cannot capture the 'dominant economic signal' or 'explicit causal chain' components that define this approach.
